what_it_does: This file is a conceptual master specification that defines the Live Intent Resolution Surface: a universal, schema-driven, generative-AI pre-specification system that turns vague human intent into an executable artifact specification through conversation, generated options, reaction capture, tags, observations, memory, assumptions, and decision closure.
observations: Defines a pre-specification workflow that runs before downstream AI execution workflows.
Uses tags, counts, structured observations, and evidence traces instead of numerical confidence scores.
Includes a 13-stage lifecycle from zero-state entry through execution handoff and feedback memory.
Provides an artifact taxonomy with 13 families and a set of universal artifact properties.
Describes a fan-out/fan-in worker architecture with many local extractors and six larger-model arbiter roles.
MVP scope deliberately limits artifact families to four: software/product idea, document/report, workflow/automation, and visual/interface concept.
Includes a self-application example that models the Live Intent Resolution Surface project using its own ontology.
Emphasizes visible assumptions via an Assumption Shelf and traceable decisions via a Decision Ledger.
Explicitly rejects chatbot, static form, simple wizard, and project-management-app metaphors.
Lists design risks, success conditions, and a recommended v0.1 implementation shape.
standouts: Core inversion: the system starts before the spec exists and helps the user discover the goal.
Reaction-based steering: the user reacts to generated options rather than writing requirements manually.
Universal entry layer → artifact-specific schema → executable handoff package pattern.
Evidence traces replace confidence scores as the basis for interpretation.
The generative UI is treated as part of the reasoning process, not just an output channel.
conventions: Numbered and hierarchical section headings with nested subsections.
Heavy use of definition lists, tables, and nested bullet lists.
Concrete examples and JSON-like state snippets illustrate abstract concepts.
Core loop and principles are repeated and refined across multiple sections.
Terminology is defined before being used in later architectural sections.
Document ends with MVP scope and a minimal recommended v0.1 implementation shape.
concerns: Extremely long and dense; may be difficult to keep internally consistent during implementation.
Some artifact categories overlap, e.g., Interactive Artifact and Software Artifact.
The proposed worker set and multi-database architecture may exceed the stated MVP scope.
Large terminology surface could become a maintenance and onboarding burden.
No explicit versioning or change log beyond the v0.1 label.
notes: A dense conceptual manifesto that tries to define an upstream intent-resolution layer before any product or technical specification is written.
questions: What is the exact schema file format and storage technology?
How are tag-ontology conflicts or tag consolidation handled?
What thresholds or rules trigger schema activation and foregrounding?
How does the system reconcile contradictory user reactions across sessions?
What is the precise serialization format for the handoff package?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the source-of-truth idea description before implementation; the file fulfills that by defining concepts, doctrine, ontology, lifecycle, and MVP shape, though it also drifts into implementation-level detail.
apparent_themes: User steering through reaction rather than manual specification
intention_signals: The file is trying to design a meta-system that sits upstream of AI builders, using structured interpretation and interactive surfaces to reduce the specification burden on users.
novelty: It positions the interface itself as an active reasoning surface and replaces confidence scores with traceable evidence tags, counts, and observations.
contribution: A comprehensive conceptual vocabulary and lifecycle for intent-resolution systems, including the reaction-based steering model, artifact taxonomy, Assumption Shelf/Decision Ledger pattern, and the universal-entry-to-schema-specific-handoff architecture.
summary_for_embedding: A master specification document defining the Live Intent Resolution Surface, a universal pre-specification system that uses schema-driven generative UI, tags, observations, memory, and decision closure to convert vague human intent into executable artifact specifications.
intention_for_embedding: To establish the conceptual source of truth and first design doctrine for a system that helps users discover and specify what to build before any downstream AI execution begins.
